verdict_reasoning: HSPD1 encodes HSP60, canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin essential for protein folding. However, HSP60 is one of the best-documented examples of a stress/tumor-induced ecto-chaperone: it is constitutively surface-displayed on many tumor cell types and is rapidly translocated to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane under heat shock, oxidative stress, and other cellular stressors, where it acts as a danger signal for innate immune recognition. Multiple surface proteomics and flow cytometry studies confirm its outer-leaflet presence on cancer cells. There are also documented cell-death-induced and activation-induced PM pools. Anti-HSP60 antibody programs targeting the cell-surface form have been explored. Thus while the dominant localization is mitochondrial, a well-characterized cancer/stress-induced ecto-pool qualifies as contextual/cell_state_induced.
